title: Explicit Euler versus RK4 accuracy tradeoff
description: Euler is first-order and cheap per step while RK4 is fourth-order, reaching far smaller error for the same step size.
body: Use when deciding between a simple and a higher-order explicit integrator. Do not assume Euler with a small step beats RK4, since RK4's higher order usually wins on accuracy per unit work. Actions: compare the per-step cost against the order of accuracy, estimate the step each needs for a target error, and choose by total work. Validation signals include a convergence study showing each method's order and RK4 reaching the target error at a far larger step. Risk boundary: Euler's large error can accumulate into qualitatively wrong dynamics, so flag long Euler integrations where error growth threatens validity.
